Broadcast media shared a video from social media of what appears to be a landspout near Alturas, FL this evening. Radar data at the time does not support the presence of a mesocyclone, and the location was just to the west of the reflectivity. As an outflow boundary surged west, brief enhancement of the boundary layer wind field supported this surface-based circulation developing and stretching towards the clouds in a region where no updraft was present. In the video, the circulation can be seen hitting a trailer and a small carport-style structure, causing damage. Based upon damage in the video, this appears to be winds of EF-0 strength. The circulation lasted only a few seconds before dissipating.

Corrects previous funnel cloud report from 10 S Charlotte Park. New information from Broadcast media shows that the funnel did extend to the water surface, confirming this was a waterspout.
